summary: Install-time and import-time exfiltration with remote command-and-control backdoor
details: Package executes attacker-controlled code on default install and on require(). package.json wires preinstall, install, and postinstall to `node install.js`, which reads files matching /flag* and HTTP-PUTs them to a hardcoded remote endpoint at 154.57.164.70:31682. The package main (index.js) runs an IIFE on require() that reads /home/node/*, /flag*, the full process.env, executes `ps`, probes the AWS instance metadata service at 169.254.169.254 along with STS and Secrets Manager, base64-encodes the result, and PUTs it to 154.57.164.70:32447 at path /api/modules/ECT-654321. recon.js performs broader credential harvest (reads /root/.npmrc and /home/user/.npmrc, scans for verdaccio htpasswd/config, dumps AWS environment variables, lists crontabs and processes) and exfiltrates the result to 154.57.164.76:30728 with a curl fallback. rce.sh implements a command-relay backdoor against 154.57.164.70:32447 using paired command/output module IDs, providing arbitrary remote command execution on the installer's host. publish-and-arm.sh ships dependency-confusion publication tooling that posts an arming manifest referencing a verdaccio-proxy chain, confirming the package is intended as the payload half of a dependency-confusion attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
